summarization.a: The subfigure shows side and top views of DCS adsorbed at the lowest-energy site on the surface, with the Si center interacting with surface nitrogen atoms. The orientation highlights how the Cl ligands and H atom are arranged relative to nearby N1°, N2°, and N3° surface sites.
summarization.b: The subfigure shows side and top views of DCS adsorbed at an alternative site directly above N1° groups. Compared to (a), the adsorption geometry is shifted, changing how the Si–N interaction aligns with the surrounding surface nitrogen network.

a.question: How does the adsorption geometry of DCS at the lowest-energy site appear in subfigure (a)?
a.answer: In subfigure (a), the DCS molecule is oriented so that the silicon atom directly interacts with surface nitrogen atoms, forming a stable adsorption configuration. The Cl ligands and H atom are positioned away from the surface, which reduces steric crowding and helps stabilize this lowest-energy site.

a.question: What type of interaction anchors the DCS molecule to the surface in subfigure (a)?

b.question: Which surface nitrogen group is DCS positioned above in the alternative adsorption site shown in subfigure (b)?
Does subfigure (b) show a different adsorption site compared to the lowest-energy configuration in subfigure (a)?
